content: Hey, I'm so exhausted today. I was up late filming a video for my new YouTube channel, 'Sweet Treats with Shiloh'. I'm really excited to share my pastry recipes with everyone.
That sounds like a great project! What inspired you to start a YouTube channel?

I've always loved sharing my passion for baking with others, and I thought it would be a great way to showcase my skills outside of the restaurant where I work. I'm a Pastry Chef at a Michelin-starred restaurant in the city.
Wow, that's impressive! I'm sure your experience will shine through in your videos. Do you have a favorite recipe that you're planning to feature soon?
Actually, I'm thinking of doing a series on beekeeping and how it relates to baking. I've been keeping bees for a few years now, and I love using their honey in my recipes.
That's fascinating! I didn't know you were a beekeeper. How did you get into that?

I started about 5 years ago, when I was living on a farm in upstate New York. I had a lot of space to keep the bees, and I just fell in love with it. Now, I have a few hives in my backyard in Brooklyn.
That's amazing. I can see how that would be a great hobby for a pastry chef. Do you find that your beekeeping and baking intersect often?

All the time! I love using the honey from my bees in my recipes, and I also appreciate the patience and attention to detail that both activities require. Speaking of which, I have to get back to training for my triathlon - I'm doing a sprint tri in July.
You're a triathlete too? That's impressive. How do you balance your training with your work and YouTube channel?

It can be tough, but I try to prioritize my time and make sure I'm getting enough rest. I usually wake up at 5am to train, and then head to the restaurant for my shift. I'm lucky to have a great team that supports me.
That sounds like a great routine. I'm sure your hard work will pay off. Good luck with your triathlon and YouTube channel - I'm excited to see how they both go!
